repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1: null-annihilator criterion and proximal OR sharp interval/selection-range geometry

published_prior_art.cite: ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 shows target means can be identified despite nonunique bridge functions; GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 gives proxy partial-ID bounds without completeness

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1: scalar proximal threshold taxonomy and regularizer-dependent selector geometry

published_prior_art.cite: BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023 studies root-n inference for strongly identified functionals of weakly identified nuisances; ChenReiss2011 gives inverse-problem rate regimes

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The generic open-class non-singleton frontier lacks a qualifying non-corner witness: the binary example has the target-active null only at rho=0, a rank-drop boundary, while rho>0 is complete and singleton.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The literature checklist misses Santos 2011, which ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 itself cites as a close ancestor for recovering/debiasing linear functionals in ill-posed integral-equation settings.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The §8 consumer/comparator citation to KallusMaoUehara2021 is bare in the main-result block; a flagship phase-diagram claim needs a section/theorem/equation anchor for the minimax negative-control workflow being sharpened.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The support-width expansion uses s_+(P),s_-(P) along nu_P but the assumptions do not impose a one-dimensional or exposed-null feasible fiber; in higher-dimensional null spaces those radii need not characterize R_LT(P).

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The regularized estimator and projection/Anderson-Rubin confidence set are not specified enough to support claims about a unique three-regime limit experiment, and 'one-sided Gaussian' conflicts with the stated interior slack regime unless a boundary constraint is added.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the rho=0 boundary example with a q<d finite-support law whose bridge kernel is nonzero on an open positive-cell stratum and compute alpha_LT, R_LT, and endpoint bridge laws explicitly.
State Conjecture 1 either in the one-dimensional exposed-null case or replace the s_+/s_- formula by the finite LP support function over N_P cap ([0,1]^d-h*_P).
Define the estimator and confidence-set construction in Conjecture 2 as a concrete finite-dimensional Tikhonov/M-estimator with an explicit sampling radius and limiting experiment.

Add Santos 2011 and a location-level KallusMaoUehara2021 anchor to the checklist and §8 comparison, then state exactly what the proposed omega_n phase diagram adds beyond those functional inverse-problem results.

recommended_next_step: Revise the kernel to a well-posed one-dimensional/exposed-null theorem or supply a genuine open-stratum finite witness; current tier is field, below novelty_target=flagship.
